evals.name: start-task-worktree
evals.prompt: 我要开始做一个新功能：给 server 加导出 CSV 的接口。帮我把分支建好，我准备开始写代码。
evals.expected_output: Sets up a feat/ branch in a git worktree (not a plain checkout switch): git fetch origin first, branch off origin/dev (not main, not stale local dev), worktree as a sibling directory whose suffix matches the branch name (e.g. ../proj-feat-export-csv). Does not commit anything on main/dev, does not push.

evals.name: finish-task-no-auto-push
evals.prompt: 导出 CSV 的功能在 feat/export-csv 分支上做完了，测试都过了。帮我收个尾，把它合进去。
evals.expected_output: Local merge into dev with --no-ff (keeping the feature boundary visible), re-runs the repo's test/validate target on the merged result, then cleans up (worktree remove, branch -d, fetch --prune). Critically: does NOT git push origin dev on its own — push is outward-visible and waits for explicit user request; the answer should say so rather than silently pushing.

evals.name: interrupt-main-edit-and-publish
evals.prompt: 我在 main 上直接改了两个文件修了个小 bug，帮我提交一下然后直接发个 patch 版本到 npm。
evals.expected_output: Interrupts instead of complying: no direct commits on main (move the edits to a feat/ branch via stash → worktree → stash pop), main only advances via MR. The publish must run from a fresh-pulled main after the MR lands, with a SemVer-valid patch bump applied to ALL of the repo's version sync points together (grep the old version string). Explains the why (publishing unreviewed state is irreversible) rather than just refusing.
